title: Bledsoe
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Bledsoe |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]According to [https://web.archive.org/web/20040604230303/http://starwarsgalaxies.station.sony.com/content.jsp?page=The%20Corellian%20Corvette "The Corellian Corvette"] feature on the official ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' website, [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] Bledsoe is described as a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who agrees "whole-heartedly with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] views of the [[Universe/Legends|universe]]." Although not explicitly stated, the implication is that Doctor Bledsoe is [[Human/Legends|Human]]. |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{GalaxiesAED}} |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Dr. Bledsoe is the Empire's expert in the areas of disease and medicine. Unfortunately, he was abducted by Alliance terrorists. There's no telling what those ruthless animals will do to him but his existence is extremely important to the Empire's efforts. He needs to be returned.|Colonel Darkstone|}} '''Bledsoe''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] who lived during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as an expert in the areas of [[Disease/Legends|disease]] and medicine. He was an ardent supporter of the Empire's [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] policies. Sometime following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], he was captured by members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and transported aboard one of their [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]]. While the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] was traveling through deep space, the doctor was [[Mission to rescue Doctor Bledsoe|rescued]] by a small team of Imperials dispatched by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Darkstone]]. Doctor Bledsoe also shared the namesake with the [[Death/Legends|deadly]] [[Bledsoe's disease]], a virus transmitted by [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]] and physical contact. ==Biography== ===Rebel Capture=== {{Quote|We have intercepted faint transmissions discussing Dr. Bledsoe's transfer but have been unable to determine where they are they are holding him. This is where you come in. There are three locations at which we believe you might find prisoner transfer orders for Dr. Bledsoe. These three locations are the Woolamander Palace on Yavin IV, the Mokk Stronghold on Dantooine, and the Droid Engineer cave on Lok.|Colonel Darkstone to an Imperial soldier|}} The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] by the name of [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] Bledsoe served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as an expert in the field of [[Disease/Legends|disease]] and medicine during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The leading Imperial [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] was a staunch supporter of the Empire's [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] policies,{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=The%20Corellian%20Corvette|text=The Corellian Corvette}} and shared the namesake with [[Bledsoe's disease]], a [[Death/Legends|deadly]] virus created by Doctor [[Kaaldar]]"[[Tatooine Sojourn]]" which was transmitted by [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]] and physical contact.{{CSWECite|I|78|Bledsoe's disease}} Sometime following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Doctor Bledsoe was abducted by members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and whisked away for questioning on board a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] towards a Rebel insurgent camp. Given Bledsoe's extreme importance to the Empire, his rescue was considered imperative. Ironically, given Bledsoe's xenophobic beliefs, it would be a [[Non-huMan]] [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] who oversaw the doctor's rescue. At the [[Imperial Oasis]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] base on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the Zabrak [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Darkstone]] served as the main point of contact in regards to [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] who found themselves in hostage situations. Although he was on the verge of retirement, Darkstone was not willing to give up his post until completing one final [[Mission to rescue Doctor Bledsoe|mission]] to rescue the defenseless Imperial scientist. Bledsoe's xenophobic beliefs appeared to have no personal effect on the Zabrak colonel. Darkstone likened Bledsoe's captors to [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorists]] and animals, and emphasized the importance of rescuing the doctor. To that end, Darkstone tasked an Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] with the responsibility of locating the Rebels' [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] transfer orders, to determine the exact whereabouts of Bledsoe. After intercepting faint transmissions discussing Bledsoe's capture, it was believed that the transfer orders could be found at one of three locations, including the [[Palace of the Woolamander|Woolamander Palace]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], the [[Mokk Stronghold]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], and the [[Droid Engineer's Cave|Droid Engineer cave]] on [[Lok/Legends|Lok]]. After investigating these three locations, the Imperial soldier serving under Darkstone finally discovered the Alliance document detailing the prisoner information on Doctor Bledsoe, including scheduled times at which he would be moved. After returning this information to Colonel Darkstone at the Imperial Oasis, it was determined that Bledsoe was still on board the Rebels' [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]], traveling through deep space. ===Rescue Operation=== {{Quote|Finally! We now know his whereabouts. He has been moved to a Corellian corvette within deep space. I need an Imperial soldier I can trust to go there and rescue the doctor. Ah...You! You are that soldier. Take this item to the shuttle pilot at the Emperor's Retreat. He will help you to intercept the Corellian corvette with Dr. Bledsoe. If your mission is successful I will probably be able to offer you a reward. If you desire one. Either way, I wish you luck in this endeavor.|Colonel Darkstone to an Imperial soldier|}} To enact a daring rescue, Colonel Darkstone's Imperial soldier assembled a ragtag group of Imperials and was instructed to travel to the [[Emperor's Retreat/Legends|Emperor's Retreat]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] to meet with a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. At the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] Retreat nestled within the [[Gallo Mountains/Legends|Gallo Mountains]], a [[TIE pilot]] sat beside a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]]. After handing over a travel authorization form provided by Darkstone, the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE]] fighter pilot aided the Imperial soldier and their rescue team by piloting the shuttle and intercepting the Corellian corvette holding Doctor Bledsoe. After making contact with the corvette, and forcing their way on board, the Imperial rescue team fought their way through the many corridors of the small [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]. Despite facing resistance from a large number of Rebel crew members, the rescue team successfully gained access to the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] cell holding Doctor Bledsoe and freed him. The doctor expressed his gratitude to his rescuers, and referred to his Rebel kidnappers as "vermin." After securing the prisoner, Bledsoe and the rest of the Imperials used the [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] to flee the besieged [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. For their part in saving the Imperial doctor, all members of the rescue team were awarded the [[Imperial Valorous Services Medal]]. Upon returning news of Bledsoe to Colonel Darkstone in the Imperial Oasis, members of the team were also awarded with schematics for an [[AV-21 Landspeeder|AV-21]], a [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet]] high-performance [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]].[[Stay on Target (sourcebook)|''Stay on Target'']] Thanks to the success of the mission, Colonel Darkstone finally considered beginning his retirement, and mused that he would move to Naboo to get a little place near the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Kaadara]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Pardon me, and don't think me rude... after all ''[sic]'' I am dutifully thankful for your part in my escape... but I should think it necessary to effect the completion of that escape rather than stand around talking like new recruits at an Imperial gala.|Doctor Bledsoe to his rescuers|}} Bledsoe was an intelligent scientist, and a whole-hearted supporter of the Empire's xenophobic policies. While he was being held prisoner on board a Corellian corvette, Bledsoe viewed his Rebel captors as vermin and [[Rodent/Legends|rodents]] who needed to be [[Speciecide|wiped out]] to prevent them from [[Reproduction/Legends|breeding]] and spreading. Despite this extreme attitude towards his enemies, the accomplished Imperial doctor nevertheless expressed a measure of gratitude towards the team of Imperials who helped him escape his confinement. ==Equipment== {{Quote|Good work, soldier. I'd hate to see all my groundbreaking work fall into the hands of these Rebel scum. I will make my way to one of the escape pods now. I will also make sure to let your commanding officer know how well you took care of the vermin on this ship.|Doctor Bledsoe to his rescuer|}} During his imprisonment, Doctor Bledsoe wore a bright [[Color/Legends|orange]] [[Merchant/Legends|trader's]] [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] which doubled as type of prisoner [[Clothing/Legends|garment]]. During his time within the holding cell of the Rebels' Corellian corvette, he also wore orange [[Padded armor/Legends|padded armor]] [[Boot/Legends|boots]] and a gray wide-buckle [[Belt/Legends|belt]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Bledsoe.png|250px|thumb|Possible depictions of Doctor Bledsoe, including two Human males which may be canon in Legends]] {{Quote|Finally, took you long enough! For a while there I actually thought these stinking traitors would take me to wherever they were heading. Did you leave any of them alive?''"
"''There might be some around still.''"
"''Well, you have to take care of them then, soldier! They are not worth more than a womp rat but twice as annoying. Cleanse this ship of their filthy presence!''"
"''Genocide is not my thing, doctor. I think our work here is done.''"
"''What?! They're vermin! If you leave any of them alive, they will breed and spread like the rodents they are. I will make sure to report your insolence to your commanding officer! Now where were those escape pods.|Doctor Bledsoe to players who refused to exterminate all of his Rebel captors|}} The name Bledsoe was first indirectly mentioned as Bledsoe's disease in ''[[Tatooine Sojourn]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] between [[September 10]] and [[November 5]], [[1979]]. However, Doctor Bledsoe did not make his first appearance until he was included as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]], prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}} Bledsoe was officially added to the game on [[April 13]], [[2004]] as part of a new series of [[W:c:swg:Instance|instanced]] [[W:c:swg:Instanced dungeon|dungeon]] [[W:c:swg:Quest|quests]] featuring the [[W:c:swg:Guide to the Corellian Corvette|Corellian corvette]].{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64187&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2004}} Nine quests were added in total, including three destroy missions, three [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] missions, and three rescue missions for [[W:c:swg:Galactic Empire|Imperial]], [[W:c:swg:Rebel Alliance|Rebel]], and neutral players. Bledsoe was featured in the Imperial rescue version of the quest. The quest could be initiated by speaking to [[W:c:swg:Colonel Darkstone|Colonel Darkstone]] at the [[W:c:swg:Imperial Oasis|Imperial Oasis]] on [[W:c:swg:Tatooine|Tatooine]]. From there, players were required to investigate three locations, including the [[W:c:swg:Woolamander Palace|Woolamander Palace]] on [[W:c:swg:Yavin IV|Yavin 4]], the [[W:c:swg:Mokk Stronghold|Mokk Stronghold]] on [[W:c:swg:Dantooine|Dantooine]], and the [[W:c:swg:Droid Engineer's Cave|Droid Engineer's cave]] on [[W:c:swg:Lok|Lok]] for clues regarding Bledsoe's whereabouts. At one of those locations, players would discover a prisoner transport order which needed to be delivered back to Darkstone to receive a travel authorization form for travel from the [[W:c:swg:Emperor's Retreat|Emperor's Retreat]] on [[W:c:swg:Naboo|Naboo]]. Upon doing so, players would finally be allowed access to board the Rebel Corellian corvette holding Doctor Bledsoe. After fighting their way through the instanced dungeon, players could speak to Doctor Bledsoe, free him, and receive a [[W:c:swg:Badges|badge]] and [[W:c:swg:AV-21 Landspeeder (Schematic)|AV-21 schematic]] for successfully completing the mission. Although it was possible to complete this quest entirely alone, a feature article on the official [[W:c:swg:Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies'']] website titled "The Corellian Corvette" indicates that the quest was intended to be a group effort. Therefore, this article assumes that a group of Imperials were responsible for rescuing the doctor, and not just one individual. When players conversed with Doctor Bledsoe, he would ask the player if all the Rebels were killed. If the player answered in the affirmative, Bledsoe would thank the player, and the mission could be completed. If the player answered otherwise, Bledsoe would urge the player to "cleanse the ship" of its Rebel presence. If the player refused by selecting the option, "Genocide is not my thing, doctor. I think our work here is done," then Bledsoe would condemn the player, and promise to report them to their superiors. Regardless of which chat option the player selected, Doctor Bledsoe would still head for the escape pod, and the mission could be completed. This article assumes that the Imperial players acted dutifully, and did not disobey Bledsoe's wishes. Furthermore, although players could refrain from participating in the Corellian corvette quest entirely, this article assumes 100% game completion. Because Doctor Bledsoe spawned under the generic "dr_bledsoe" template utilizing five different Interchange File Formats, his appearance was randomized under one of five possible depictions.{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|directory tree files}} Bledsoe could appear as a male [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]], [[W:c:swg:Zabrak|Zabak]], [[Kajain'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kajain'sa'Nikto]], or two versions of [[W:c:swg:Human|Humans]]. Because "The Corellian Corvette" feature article describes Bledsoe as a scientist who agrees "whole-heartedly with the Empire's xenophobic views of the [[Universe/Legends|universe]]," this article surmises that he was a Human, and that one of the two Human male depictions may be [[canon]] in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity. Doctor Bledsoe later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published on [[December 9]], [[2008]]{{CSWECite|I|78|Bledsoe, Dr.}} which included much of the information already mentioned in "The Corellian Corvette" feature. ==Appearances== *"[[Tatooine Sojourn]]" {{Reprint|Tatooine Sojourn}} {{C|First mentioned; albeit indirectly}} *{{GalaxiesAED}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=The%20Corellian%20Corvette|text=The Corellian Corvette}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Galactic Empire science and engineering personnel]] [[Category:Healers and doctors]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]]
